req.title: Create medication request (boundary)
req.statement: A prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest; requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification. Boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers three distinct test axes: (1) a happy-path POST by a credentialed prescriber for a non-controlled substance (EP – valid partition), (2) a forbidden path where a prescriber attempts to issue a CII controlled-substance request without schedule verification (NEG/DT – decision table row: CII + no schedule cert = 403), and (3) boundary values on the numeric dosage quantity and the ISO-8601 authoredOn timestamp (BVA – min/max quantity, midnight timestamp edge). A fourth State Transition test is added to verify the resource moves from 'active' to 'completed' after pharmacy acknowledgement (STC). All tests use a shared <prescriber_auth_fixture> that returns a bearer token and a <base_url> fixture pointing at the fhir-lite test instance.

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_non_controlled_success
preconditions: Medication is non-controlled (Schedule V or unscheduled)
actions: Construct a MedicationRequest payload with a valid non-controlled medicationCodeableConcept
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field (server-assigned)
Pharmacy notification event recorded (location header or notification stub)
trace: R-HLT-033: prescribing clinician shall issue a MedicationRequest
R-HLT-033: expected outcome – request stored; pharmacy notified
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_medication_request_non_controlled_success(base_url, prescriber_auth_fixture):
    token = prescriber_auth_fixture(role="prescriber")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Amoxicillin 500 MG"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "dosageInstruction": [{"doseAndRate": [{"doseQuantity": {"value": 1, "unit": "tablet"}}]}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "MedicationRequest"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert "id" in body

tests.name: test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_forbidden
preconditions: Medication is Schedule II controlled substance (CII)
No schedule verification credential attached to the token or request
actions: Construct a MedicationRequest payload with a CII medicationCodeableConcept (e.g., oxycodone)
Omit any scheduleVerification extension or header
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an OperationOutcome or error message referencing schedule verification
trace: R-HLT-033: requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification
R-HLT-033: error pathway – 403 without schedule verification for CII
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_cii_medication_request_without_schedule_verification_forbidden(
        base_url, prescriber_auth_fixture):
    token = prescriber_auth_fixture(role="prescriber", schedule_verified=False)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049683", "display": "Oxycodone 5 MG (CII)"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "dosageInstruction": [{"doseAndRate": [{"doseQuantity": {"value": 1, "unit": "tablet"}}]}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    issue_text = str(body)
    assert "schedule" in issue_text.lower() or "verification" in issue_text.lower(), \
        "Error body should reference schedule verification"

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_boundary_dosage_quantity_and_timestamp
actions: Parameterize over boundary values: dosage quantity 0.001 (min positive), 9999 (system max), and authoredOn at midnight UTC '2024-01-01T00:00:00Z'
Assert expected status per boundary rule
expected: quantity=0.001 and midnight timestamp -> HTTP 201 (min-valid boundary accepted)
quantity=9999 and midnight timestamp -> HTTP 201 (max-valid boundary accepted)
quantity=0 -> HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity (zero dose rejected)
quantity=-1 -> HTTP 422 Unprocessable Entity (negative dose rejected)
trace: R-HLT-033: boundary cases on timestamps and numeric elements must be tested
R-HLT-033: expected outcome – request stored for valid boundary values
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

@pytest.mark.parametrize("qty,authored_on,expected_status", [
    (0.001, "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", 201),
    (9999,  "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", 201),
    (0,     "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", 422),
    (-1,    "2024-01-01T00:00:00Z", 422),
])
def test_create_medication_request_boundary_dosage_quantity_and_timestamp(
        base_url, prescriber_auth_fixture, qty, authored_on, expected_status):
    token = prescriber_auth_fixture(role="prescriber")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Amoxicillin 500 MG"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "authoredOn": authored_on,
        "dosageInstruction": [{"doseAndRate": [{"doseQuantity": {"value": qty, "unit": "tablet"}}]}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == expected_status, \
        f"qty={qty} authoredOn={authored_on}: expected {expected_status}, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"

tests.name: test_medication_request_state_transition_active_to_completed
preconditions: A MedicationRequest in status 'active' has been created and its id is known
actions: POST /MedicationRequest to create an active request and capture id
Simulate pharmacy acknowledgement by PATCHing status to 'completed'
expected: Initial POST returns HTTP 201 with status='active'
No intermediate states skipped (active -> completed direct transition allowed by FHIR state machine)
trace: R-HLT-033: expected outcome – request stored; pharmacy notified
R-HLT-033: state transition active -> completed upon pharmacy acknowledgement
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_medication_request_state_transition_active_to_completed(base_url, prescriber_auth_fixture):
    token = prescriber_auth_fixture(role="prescriber")
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049502", "display": "Amoxicillin 500 MG"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "dosageInstruction": [{"doseAndRate": [{"doseQuantity": {"value": 1, "unit": "tablet"}}]}]
    }
    create_resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert create_resp.status_code == 201
    resource_id = create_resp.json()["id"]
    patch_headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/json-patch+json"}
    patch_body = [{"op": "replace", "path": "/status", "value": "completed"}]
    patch_resp = requests.patch(
        f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest/{resource_id}", json=patch_body, headers=patch_headers)
    assert patch_resp.status_code == 200, f"PATCH failed: {patch_resp.text}"
    get_resp = requests.get(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest/{resource_id}", headers=headers)
    assert get_resp.status_code == 200
    assert get_resp.json().get("status") == "completed"

tests.name: test_create_medication_request_decision_table_cii_with_schedule_verification_allowed
preconditions: Bearer token includes schedule_verified=True credential for CII substances
Medication is Schedule II controlled substance
actions: Attach schedule verification extension to the payload
POST /MedicationRequest with Authorization header carrying schedule-verified token
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains an id field
trace: R-HLT-033: requests for controlled substances shall require schedule verification
R-HLT-033: decision table row – CII + schedule verified = 201 allowed
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_create_medication_request_decision_table_cii_with_schedule_verification_allowed(
        base_url, prescriber_auth_fixture):
    token = prescriber_auth_fixture(role="prescriber", schedule_verified=True)
    headers = {"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}", "Content-Type": "application/fhir+json"}
    payload = {
        "resourceType": "MedicationRequest",
        "status": "active",
        "intent": "order",
        "extension": [{
            "url": "http://fhir-lite.example/StructureDefinition/schedule-verification",
            "valueBoolean": True
        }],
        "medicationCodeableConcept": {
            "coding": [{"system": "http://www.nlm.nih.gov/research/umls/rxnorm",
                        "code": "1049683", "display": "Oxycodone 5 MG (CII)"}]
        },
        "subject": {"reference": "Patient/test-patient-001"},
        "requester": {"reference": "Practitioner/test-prescriber-001"},
        "authoredOn": "2024-06-01T08:00:00Z",
        "dosageInstruction": [{"doseAndRate": [{"doseQuantity": {"value": 1, "unit": "tablet"}}]}]
    }
    resp = requests.post(f"{base_url}/MedicationRequest", json=payload, headers=headers)
    assert resp.status_code == 201, f"Expected 201, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("resourceType") == "MedicationRequest"
    assert body.get("status") == "active"
    assert "id" in body
